headline: Construction Material Costs Rise 28% Squeezing Developers
context: Construction material costs in Malaysia rose 28% between 2023 and 2025 squeezing developer margins and delaying affordable housing.

cards.big: Construction material costs rose 28% in 2 years squeezing developers
cards.sub: Steel cement and sand price increases adding RM35,000-50,000 per housing unit

cards.big: Steel bar prices rose from RM2,400 to RM3,200 per tonne since 2023
cards.sub: Malaysia imports 45% of construction steel exposing market to global volatility

cards.big: Ready-mixed concrete costs up 22% due to sand shortage in Peninsula states
cards.sub: 4 states imposed sand export bans driving prices up further in 2025

cards.big: Developers delayed 180 affordable housing projects citing unviable margins
cards.sub: Government refused to extend price control beyond existing cement subsidy

cards.big: Material costs test whether affordable housing targets survive market price pressures
28% material cost rise squeezes developers — but the cost is ultimately passed to buyers and renters.
